evidence:
From I-HY_2022_14-SCT-00: "# SCT 19 Fee Percentage
The fee percentage to be inserted by the tenderer in the Contract Data Part two shall be within a range from the minimum fee percentage to the cap of fee percentage as stated in the Contract Data Part two. Any error found should be corrected as per the principles and rules contained in Appendix F to the General Conditions of Tender as mentioned in General Conditions of Tender Clause GCT 11." From ECC HK_SCT19_20231115.docx: "Clause | Remarks/Guidelines
SCT 19  Fee Percentage | SCT 19  Fee Percentage
The fee percentage to be inserted by the tenderer in the Contract Data Part two shall be within a range from the minimum fee percentage to the cap of fee percentage as stated in the Contract Data Part two. Any error found should be corrected as per the principles and rules contained in Appendix [ ]# to the General Conditions of Tender as mentioned in General Conditions of Tender Clause GCT [11] #. | DEVB memo ref. DEVB(W"
reasoning: The tender clause specifies 'Appendix F' and 'GCT 11' explicitly, whereas the standard clause leaves these as placeholders ('[ ]#' and '[11] #'). This is a documented refinement rather than a contradiction.

evidence:
From I-HY_2022_14-SCT-00: "# SCT 10 Funding Approval
The tenderers shall note that this tender exercise is held before the necessary funds for this contract have been approved. The tenderers shall also note that the Client reserves the right to cancel this tender exercise for not having the necessary funds approved; and that the tenderers will accordingly be notified if the necessary funds are not approved." From ECC HK_SCT10_20231115.docx: "Clause | Remarks/Guidelines
SCT 10 Funding Approval | SCT 10 Funding Approval
The tenderers shall note that this tender exercise is held before the necessary funds for the contract have been approved.  The tenderers shall also note that the Client reserves the right to cancel this tender exercise for not having the necessary funds approved; and that the tenderers will accordingly be notified if the necessary funds are not approved. | To be used if funding has not been approved at the time of ten"
reasoning: The tender clause matches the standard clause word-for-word and includes all required elements regarding funding approval and the Client's right to cancel.

reasoning: The STANDARD clause explicitly states it applies 'if joint venture is not allowed to tender' and requires insertion of approved contractor categories per the Tender Notice. evidence:
From I-HY_2022_14-GCT-00: "# SCT 17 Activity Schedule
(1) The tenderer shall submit an activity schedule fully priced as to each of the activities, extended, cast, totalled, completed and signed as required in Clause GCT 4 of the General Conditions of Tender. The priced activity schedule shall contain a summary (“Grand Summary of the activity schedule”) at the end stating the tendered total of the Prices. Failure to submit this Grand Summary by the tenderer shall render its tender invalid.
(2) A sample form of activity sc" From ECC HK_SCT17_20240318_clean_r1.docx: "Clause | Clause | Remarks/Guidelines
SCT 17  Activity Schedule | SCT 17  Activity Schedule | SCT 17  Activity Schedule
(1) | The tenderer shall submit an activity schedule fully priced as to each of the activities, extended, cast, totalled, completed and signed as required in Clause GCT 4 of the General Conditions of Tender. | Applicable for Options A and C
(2) | The priced activity schedule, which shall contain a summary (“Grand Summary of the activity schedule”) stating the tendered total of t"
reasoning: The tender clause introduces a stricter consequence—rendering the tender invalid for missing the Grand Summary—whereas the standard allows correction per GCT 11. It also replaces the mandatory form requirement with a non-binding sample and shifts responsibility to the tenderer, contradicting the standard’s requirement to use the specified Appendix form.
